commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `persist-credentials: false` to CodeQL checkout steps and also adds `.editorconfig`, `.node-version`, and broader `.gitattributes` rules.

Reproducibility: yes. for the configuration gap: current main has CodeQL checkout steps without `persist-credentials: false`, and the pinned checkout action defaults persisted credentials to true. I did not run the workflows themselves.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor action for proof and branch cleanup; this should not enter an automated repair lane while the real-behavior proof gate is missing.

target.title: fix(ci): add persist-credentials: false to CodeQL checkout steps
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `persist-credentials: false` to 18 checkout steps across 4 CodeQL workflow files that were missing it.

## Why

The `actions/checkout` action persists GitHub token credentials in the local git config by default. If a later step is compromised, it can use those credentials to push to the repo or access other resources. For read-only jobs like CodeQL analysis, credentials should be explicitly disabled.

This follows the same pattern already present in `ci.yml` checkout steps (lines 84, 307, 1427, 1446).

## Files Changed

| File | Steps Fixed |
|------|-------------|
| `codeql-android-critical-security.yml` | 1 |
| `codeql-critical-quality.yml` | 15 |
| `codeql-macos-critical-security.yml` | 1 |
| `codeql.yml` | 1 |

All CodeQL jobs are read-only (analysis only) — none push code or create releases.

## Test Plan

- [ ] Verify YAML syntax is valid (confirmed locally with PyYAML)
- [ ] Verify CodeQL workflows still trigger and complete successfully on next scheduled run
- [ ] Confirm no other checkout steps in these files are missing `persist-credentials: false`
